stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The Khmer Empire lasted about 600 years (802 CE to 1431 CE).
  • The Khmer controlled an area spanning about 1 million square kilometers.
  • This area included present-day Cambodia and parts of Thailand, Laos, and Vietnam.
  • The empire's capital was the city of Angkor.
  • Angkor was located near present-day Siem Reap, Cambodia.
stem: The student wants to specify the location of Angkor. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Khmer Empire began in 802 CE and ended in 1431 CE.
Angkor, the capital of the Khmer Empire, was located near present-day Siem Reap, Cambodia.
The Khmer controlled an area of about 1 million square kilometers, including present-day Cambodia.
The capital of the Khmer Empire, which spanned about 1 million square kilometers, was Angkor.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence specifies the location of Angkor, noting that it was located near present-day Siem Reap, Cambodia.

Choice A is incorrect because the sentence explains when the Khmer Empire began and ended; it doesn't specify the location of Angkor. Choice C is incorrect because it emphasizes the size of the Khmer Empire; it doesn't specify the location of Angkor. Choice D is incorrect because the sentence emphasizes that Angkor was the capital of the Khmer Empire; it doesn't specify Angkor's location.
